section_miscategorizations.reason: This appears to be a dictation error; vitals should refer to the patient, not the doctor. However, it is still clearly an objective vital sign, so the section is correct despite the wording issue.
Same as above: wording says 'doctor' but context indicates a patient vital sign; section is appropriate.
Same dictation issue; still an objective vital sign, so section placement is acceptable.

section_miscategorizations.text: The clinician orders a pulmonary function test (PFT).
section_miscategorizations.reason: Orders and planned tests belong in the Plan section, not in the Assessment.

section_miscategorizations.text: The clinician orders a PET CT.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Imaging orders are management actions and should be documented in the Plan, not the Assessment.

section_miscategorizations.text: The clinician suggests video assisted thoracoscopy removal of the nodule.
section_miscategorizations.reason: A surgical recommendation is a management plan item and belongs in the Plan section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient takes methotrexate seven and a half milligrams weekly.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is an existing medication regimen, not a new order or change; per instructions, current medications belong in the Subjective section.
